self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can present queue lifecycle state more truthfully when broken completed entries are explicitly marked as inconsistent completion instead of rendered as clean completion.
self_improvement.mechanism: The adopted slice retains one derived queue lifecycle-status guard and operator-facing warning for broken completed entries.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP opener, broken queue entries still surfaced the raw completed lifecycle tag without any explicit stale-completion warning.
self_improvement.expected_effect: The queue surface will stop overstating completed work for broken entries while preserving the raw queue status as evidence.

decision.rationale: The slice is small, operator-visible, and directly aligned with the queue lifecycle mismatch bottleneck.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
